zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas de la Lee於1252年出生在英國史洛普爾特爾福德時，他的父親Sir Thomas de la Lee13歲，母親Petronilla Corbet14歲。他於1273年與Petronilla Stanton在英國英格蘭史洛普爾斯坦頓 武朋 海因 希斯結婚。他們至少是7個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1327年在英國格盧斯特斯爾享年76歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Lee
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sang, Jae, Jong, Jung, Sung, Yong, Kyung, Seung, Dong, Kwang, Myung.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow or a patch of arable land, Middle English lee, lea, from Old English lēa, dative case (used after a preposition) of lēah, which originally meant ‘wood or glade’.

English: habitational name from any of the many places in England named with Old English lēah ‘wood, glade’, including Lee in Buckinghamshire, Essex, Hampshire, Kent, and Shropshire, and Lea in Cheshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, and Wiltshire.

variants.name: Ley
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
German: from a medieval personal name, Eloy, from Latin Eligius (see Eligio ), a Christian saint's name.
German (Rhineland): topographic name from Middle High German leie ‘rock, stone, slate’, or a habitational name from any of several places called with this word. Compare Leier .

variants.name: Lees
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English lees ‘fields, arable land’, plural of lee (see Lee ), or from Middle English lese ‘pasture, meadow’ (Old English lǣs).

English: habitational name fromLeece or Lees in Lancashire, or Leese in Cheshire, all named from OldEnglish lēas ‘woodland clearings’ (plural oflēah), or from Leece in Cumbria, which was probably namedwith a Celtic word, lïss ‘hall, court, the principalhouse in a district’.
English: variant of   Leece   1.

variants.name: Lay
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Scottish: shortened form of   McClay  .
German: habitational name from any of the places so named, in the Rhineland near Koblenz and in Bavaria, from lay(h), a word meaning ‘stone, rock, slate’.

variants.name: Laye
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

variants.name: Lie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Oystein, Sven, Thor.  German Hans, Gunther, Hilde.  Korean Han, Chang Ho, Chen, Chun, Hok, Jung, Kam, Liang, Lie, Ling, Shuen, Yung.
Norwegian: variant of Li 11, a habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads named Li, from Old Norse hlíth ‘mountain slope, hillside’. In Norway this is the most frequent spelling.
German: unexplained.

variants.name: Layman
meanings: English: topographic name for a dweller by a woodland clearing or a meadow, from Middle English ley + man. Compare Lee .
English: nickname from layman, a word denoting an uneducated person.
Americanized form of German   Lehmann  .

variants.name: Lea
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 李 and 利, see   Li   1 and 3.

variants.name: Lye
meanings: English: variant of   Lee   1, a topographic name for someone who lived near a patch of arable land, or a habitational name from Lye in Herefordshire (with the same etymology).

Norwegian: habitational name from a farmstead in Rogaland named Lye, Old Norse Lýgi meaning ‘alliance, covenant’, used to denote a place sanctified by such an agreement, such as a court or council meeting place.
Altered form of German   Ley  .

variants.name: Saelee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Chan, Kao, Nai, Chai, Cheng, Chien, Foo, Hin, Muang, Pao, Seng, Song, Sou.
Southeast Asian (Iu Mien): from the name of the Lee clan of the Iu Mien people from Laos + the Thai prefix sae, denoting a clan. Compare Lee 8.

variants.name: Leigh
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in at least sixteen counties, but especially Leigh in Lancashire) named either with the nominative case of Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ (see Lee ) or with lēage, a late dative form of this word (see Lye ).
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas de la Lee was born in 1252, in Telford, Shropshire, England, his father, Sir Thomas de la Lee, was 13 and his mother, Petronilla Corbet, was 14. He married Petronilla Stanton in 1273, in Stanton upon Hine Heath, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons. He died about 1327, in Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 76.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas de la Lee est né en 1252, à Telford, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas de la Lee, avait 13 ans et sa mère, Petronilla Corbet, avait 14 ans. Il a épousé Petronilla Stanton en 1273, à Stanton upon Hine Heath, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils. Il est décédé environ 1327, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 76 ans.

Lui sposato Petronilla Stanton nel 1273, nel Stanton upon Hine Heath, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli. ja.lifeSummary: Thomas de la Leeが1252年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアテルフォードで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas de la Leeは13歳，母Petronilla Corbetは14歳であった。1273年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアスタントン ウプオン ヒン ヒースでPetronilla Stantonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子の親であった。彼は約1327年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアで76歳で死去した。

ko.Stanton upon Hine Heath, Shropshire dist: 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 스탠턴 업 이인 히스
ko.Roden, Stanton upon Hine Heath, Shropshi: 영국 슈럽실르 스탠턴 업 이인 히스
ko.Stanton upon Hine Heath, Shropshire, Eng: 영국 슈럽실르 스탠턴 업 이인 히스
ko.Saint Andrew's Church, Stanton upon Hine: 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 스탠턴 업 이인 히스
ko.lifeSummary: Thomas de la Lee(이)가 1252년 영국 슈럽실르 텔퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas de la Lee(은)는 13세였고, 그의 어머니 Petronilla Corbet(은)는 14세였다. 그는 1273년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 스탠턴 업 이인 히스에서 Petronilla Stanton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1327년 영국 그루스스터실르에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas de la Lee nasceu em 1252, em Telford, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas de la Lee, tinha 13 anos e sua mãe, Petronilla Corbet, tinha 14 anos. Ele casou-se com Petronilla Stanton em 1273, em Stanton upon Hine Heath, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1327, em Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, com 76 anos.

ru.Roden, Stanton upon Hine Heath, Shropshi: стантон упон хаин хит, шропшир, Англия

ru.Saint Andrew's Church, Stanton upon Hine: стантон упон хаин хит, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas de la Lee родился в 1252 г. в местности телфорд, шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas de la Lee было 13 лет, а матери, которую звали Petronilla Corbet, 14. Он женился на женщине по имени Petronilla Stanton в 1273 г. в местности стантон упон хаин хит, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей. Он умер около 1327 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 76 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas de la Lee nació en 1252, en Telford, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas de la Lee, tenía 13 años y su mamá, Petronilla Corbet, tenía 14 años. Se casó con Petronilla Stanton en 1273, en Stanton upon Hine Heath, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos. Murió aproximadamente 1327, en Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 76 años.
